description_long: This is a slender, elongated pitchfork with a metallic blue finish, featuring three upward-curving tines at the top. The tines are evenly spaced and have pointed tips, suitable for piercing or lifting materials. The shaft is straight and tapers slightly towards the bottom, with a subtle spiral or twisted design near the lower end, adding both visual interest and grip texture. The handle appears to be integrated with the shaft, lacking any additional grip covering or ergonomic features. The entire object has a uniform color and material, suggesting it is made from a single piece of metal. There are no visible signs of rust, wear, or damage, and the surface appears smooth and hard.
description: A sleek, blue metallic pitchfork with three pointed tines and a straight, slightly twisted shaft. The design is uniform and modern, with no visible wear or grip covering.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three tines clearly, with the spiral detail on the lower shaft visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the tines and shaft, with the spiral detail appearing as a subtle ridge.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the three tines and the shaft's spiral detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the shaft and tines, with the spiral detail visible as a ridge.
